acceptance_targets: [ch14-booker-001] In the revised corridor/prep-room sequence, Lena must issue at least one specific forensic or detention directive naming Hollis, and the text must show a concrete outcome (compliance or explicit refusal with stated authority). Pass only if Hollis's evidentiary status is clear before the chapter ends.

[ch14-booker-002] Pass only if the revised prep-room scene contains an explicit reason Eleanor chooses disclosure now, plus one named risk she accepts by giving Lena the code, before the combination is spoken or written.

[ch14-craft-001] Pass only if the revised text shows at least one completed, observable custody consequence for Deshawn on-page (not reported secondhand), and at least one embodied effect (breath, speech, bleeding, or posture change) before Lena leaves that lane.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised corridor/prep-room sequence, Lena must issue at least one specific forensic or detention directive naming Hollis, and the text must show a concrete outcome (compliance or explicit refusal with stated authority). Pass only if Hollis's evidentiary status is clear before the chapter ends.
findings.problem: The chapter plants actionable incriminating material around Hollis (proximity at the compromised corridor, victim clutching torn black-polo material, Eleanor naming him as mover, blood on his cuff) but Lena never attempts an on-page evidentiary action against him. In a chain-of-custody novel, that gap reads as causal evasion.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:167 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:395, add explicit evidence-preservation action (photo, bag, swab, or witness hold order) tied to Hollis. At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:215, show Clay or another authority explicitly blocking that action with a stated legal/command rationale so the obstacle is causal, not omitted.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the revised prep-room scene contains an explicit reason Eleanor chooses disclosure now, plus one named risk she accepts by giving Lena the code, before the combination is spoken or written.
findings.problem: The witness-to-document bridge (safe location plus combination plus Hollis allegation) arrives too frictionlessly: the guard barrier collapses in one beat and Eleanor's disclosure lands as plot delivery before a fully earned leverage turn.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:319 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:369 to add one clear leverage pivot before disclosure (for example: Eleanor verifies chain protection, demands a condition, or reacts to imminent command interference at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:381). Keep the safe details, but make the choice to disclose visibly costly.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the revised text shows at least one completed, observable custody consequence for Deshawn on-page (not reported secondhand), and at least one embodied effect (breath, speech, bleeding, or posture change) before Lena leaves that lane.
findings.problem: Sanitization/timidity drift in the Deshawn detention thread: the prose reaches concrete police violence, then exits the scene before custody consequences fully materialize on-page. That cut defuses institutional brutality at the moment the narrative has earned specificity.
findings.rewrite_direction: Extend snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:291 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:305 (or directly after snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:431) with one immediate custody consequence rendered in scene: rights advisement denial/delay with time marker, medical triage denial, booking instruction, or counsel obstruction. Keep physical and legal consequence in the same beat before pivoting back to Eleanor.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the version that preserves uncomfortable specificity and character accountability; do not replace difficult scenes with procedural summary, and do not mute class, race, sexuality, or institutional cruelty when those forces are actively shaping choices.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene with violence, racism, grief, or moral compromise is rendered in abstract or polite shorthand, revise to restore specific bodily, social, and material consequence before accepting the draft.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in contemporary Mississippi speech; uncontracted forms are reserved for formality, emphasis, or social performance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: rotate structural textures across chapters: procedural surge, quiet domestic interval, document-driven slow burn, dread preparation, aftermath inventory, and information-turn chapters; avoid repeating scene count plus dialogue density pattern in adjacent chapters.
prose_style_profile.diction: literary but grounded; precise legal and forensic terms when needed, balanced by lived vernacular and local material detail.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored primarily to Lena, with brief controlled expansions only when factual clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternate compressed procedural sentences with longer sensory-emotional lines so pressure and reflection keep rebalancing.
